name: Survivor
description: The long-running CBS reality-competition show about castaways outwitting, outplaying, and outlasting

questions.q: Who hosts the CBS reality competition Survivor?

source.notes: Probst has won four Primetime Emmys for Outstanding Host of a Reality Program.

questions.q: How much money does the Sole Survivor win?

source.notes: Winners at War (Season 40) upped the prize to $2M for that season only.

questions.q: Who won the very first season of U.S. Survivor in 2000?

source.notes: Played a pioneering strategic alliance game.

questions.q: In what year did Survivor first premiere in the United States?

source.notes: Kicked off the modern U.S. reality-TV boom.

questions.q: On which U.S. network does Survivor air?

source.notes: Has been a flagship CBS unscripted show since 2000.

questions.q: What phrase does Jeff Probst say as he extinguishes a torch at Tribal Council?

source.notes: The show's most iconic catchphrase.

questions.q: How many days did contestants typically spend on the island during classic Survivor seasons?

source.notes: The standard length from Season 1 until Season 41.

questions.q: Which Survivor legend became the first two-time champion, winning Pearl Islands and Heroes vs. Villains?

source.notes: Self-styled 'Queen Stays Queen.'

questions.q: Where was Survivor: Borneo (Season 1) filmed?

source.notes: An uninhabited island in the South China Sea.

questions.q: Which season introduced the Hidden Immunity Idol to the game?

source.notes: First found by Gary Hogeboom.

questions.q: Which Season 40 all-winners season was won by Tony Vlachos in 2020?

source.notes: Vlachos's second win, after Cagayan.

questions.q: Which British TV producer created the original concept that became Survivor?

source.notes: Created the Swedish original Expedition Robinson (1997). Mark Burnett bought the U.S. rights.

questions.q: Since Season 33, Survivor has been filmed almost exclusively in which country?

source.notes: Production infrastructure is now permanent there.

questions.q: Which 2017 twist forces a fire-making challenge among the Final Four?

source.notes: First used on Heroes v. Healers v. Hustlers.

questions.q: Which Season 22 twist gave eliminated players a chance to re-enter the game?

source.notes: Aired in 2011 and was won by Rob Mariano.

questions.q: Which Season 7 entry brought a pirate-themed 'Outcast Tribe' comeback?

source.notes: Won by Sandra Diaz-Twine.

questions.q: Which season divided tribes by ethnicity, sparking controversy in 2006?

source.notes: Tribes were split into Black, White, Asian, and Latino.

questions.q: Rob Mariano ('Boston Rob') finally won the title on which season after multiple prior attempts?

source.notes: His fourth time playing.

questions.q: What is the name of the iconic Survivor opening theme music?

source.notes: Composed by Russ Landau.

questions.q: Which Season 13 winner of Survivor: Cook Islands is often praised for his 'Strategy God' gameplay?

source.notes: Won with a unanimous final-tribal vote.

questions.q: Which Season 15 winner prevailed in China with a masterful final tribal performance?

source.notes: Flight attendant from Utah.

questions.q: Starting with Season 41 in 2021, how many days are now in a standard Survivor season?

source.notes: Shortened permanently after the pandemic production break.
